title: श्री साई कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री साई कवचम् ॥

ॐ श्री सद्गुरु साईनाथाय नमः।

साईश मे शिरः पातु ललाटं सद्गुरुः सदा।
नेत्रे पातु दयानिधिः श्रोत्रे पातु कृपानिधिः॥ १॥

नासां पातु जगद्गुरुः मुखं शिरडीशः सदा।
जिह्वां पातु सत्यवाक् कण्ठं पातु दयार्णवः॥ २॥

स्कन्धौ पातु महायोगी बाहू पातु तपोनिधिः।
हस्तौ पातु वरप्रदः हृदयं पातु दीनबन्धुः॥ ३॥

वक्षः पातु कृपासिन्धुः उदरं पातु अन्नदाता।
नाभिं भिक्षुकवेषधृक् कटिं पातु विरक्तधीः॥ ४॥

ऊरू पातु जगत्पिता जानुनी पातु शान्तमूर्तिः।
जङ्घे पातु धुनिप्रभुः पादौ पातु समाधिस्थः॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां पातु सदा साईः दक्षिणे श्रद्धादेवता।
पश्चिमे सबुरीनाथः उत्तरे प्रेमसागरः॥ ६॥

सर्वतः सर्वदा पातु श्री साईनाथ सद्गुरुः।
इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा साई स्तोत्रं न सिध्यति॥ ७॥

यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय साईकवचमुत्तमम्।
सर्वसंकटमुक्तो भूत् साईलोकं स गच्छति॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री साई कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sai Kavacham ||

Om Shri Sadguru Sainathaya Namah |

Saisha Me Shirah Patu Lalatam Sadguruh Sada |
Netre Patu Dayanidhih Shrotre Patu Kripanidhih || 1 ||

Nasam Patu Jagadguruh Mukham Shirdishah Sada |
Jihvam Patu Satyavak Kantham Patu Dayarnavah || 2 ||

Skandhau Patu Mahayogi Bahu Patu Taponidhih |
Hastau Patu Varapradah Hridayam Patu Dinabandhuh || 3 ||

Vakshah Patu Kripasindhuh Udaram Patu Annadata |
Nabhim Bhikshukaveshadrik Katim Patu Viraktadhih || 4 ||

Uru Patu Jagatpita Januni Patu Shantamurtih |
Janghe Patu Dhuniprabhuh Padau Patu Samadhisthah || 5 ||

Prachyam Patu Sada Saih Dakshine Shraddhadevata |
Pashchime Saburinathah Uttare Premasagarah || 6 ||

Sarvatah Sarvada Patu Shri Sainatha Sadguruh |
Idam Kavacham Ajnatva Sai Stotram Na Sidhyati || 7 ||

Yah Pathet Pratarutthaya Sai Kavacham Uttamam |
Sarva Sankata Mukto Bhut Sailokam Sa Gachchhati || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Sai Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
साईश मेरे मस्तक की, सद्गुरु ललाट की, दयानिधि नेत्रों की और कृपानिधि कानों की रक्षा करें।

जगद्गुरु नासिका की, शिरडीश मुख की, सत्यवाक् जिह्वा की और दयार्णव कण्ठ की रक्षा करें।

महायोगी कन्धों की, तपोनिधि भुजाओं की, वरप्रद हाथों की और दीनबन्धु हृदय की रक्षा करें।

कृपासिन्धु वक्ष की, अन्नदाता उदर की, भिक्षुकवेषधारी नाभि की और विरक्तधी कटि की रक्षा करें।

साई पूर्व में, श्रद्धा देवता दक्षिण में, सबूरीनाथ पश्चिम में और प्रेमसागर उत्तर में रक्षा करें।

श्री साईनाथ सद्गुरु सर्वत्र सर्वदा रक्षा करें। यह कवच जाने बिना साई स्तोत्र सिद्ध नहीं होता।
benefits: Divine protective armor of Shirdi Sai Baba for complete protection
Invokes Sai as Sadguru, Jagadguru, Dayasindhu and Premasagar
Unique kavach using Sai Baba's attributes as divine names
Invokes protection from all dangers and obstacles in life
Ideal for daily Sai worship and Thursday recitation
description: Sai Kavacham — श्री साई कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Shree Sai Baba of Shirdi. Attributed to Sai Devotional Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke Sai as Sadguru, Jagadguru, Dayasindhu and Premasagar and grant protection from all dangers and obstacles in life. It is also said to unique kavach using Sai Baba's attributes as divine names. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima for enhanced blessings. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sai Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sai Kavacham and what makes it distinctive among kavachams?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Kavacham is an eight-verse Sanskrit armour-prayer (kavacha) dedicated to Shirdi Sai Baba (1838-1918), the celebrated saint of Shirdi in Maharashtra revered across Hindu and Sufi traditions. Composed in Anushtup metre and circulated within the modern Sai devotional tradition, it is distinctive because it does not invoke a Puranic deity with classical iconography but rather invokes Sai Baba under his historically recorded epithets and attributes — Sadguru, Jagadguru, Shirdishah (Lord of Shirdi), Dayasindhu (ocean of compassion), Bhikshukavesha-dhrik (the one in mendicant garb), Dhuni-prabhuh (lord of the sacred fire), and Samadhistha (the one in samadhi at his Shirdi tomb). (Sources: Shri Sai Satcharitra tradition; Sai Devotional compilations.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Sai Kavacham structure its protection of the body?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacham systematically envelops the chanter's body part by part with Sai's protective grace under different epithets. Verses 1-2 protect the head, forehead, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, tongue and throat under Saisha, Sadguru, Dayanidhi (ocean of mercy), Kripanidhi (treasure of grace), Jagadguru, Shirdishah, Satyavak (truth-speaker) and Dayarnava (ocean of compassion). Verses 3-5 protect shoulders to feet under Mahayogi, Taponidhi, Varaprada, Dinabandhu, Kripasindhu, Annadata (food-giver), Jagatpita (father of the world), Shantamurti, Dhuni-prabhu and Samadhistha. Verse 6 then protects the four directions under Sai, Shraddha-devata, Saburinath and Premasagara — preserving Sai's twin teaching of Shraddha (faith) and Saburi (patience). (Sources: Sai Devotional Tradition; Sai Satcharitra commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Shraddha and Saburi in the Sai Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 6 of the Sai Kavacham invokes Shraddha-devata as the protector in the southern direction and Saburinath (lord of patience) as the protector in the western direction — directly enshrining Sai Baba's most famous teaching. Throughout his life Sai Baba would tell visitors that all he asked for as dakshina was Shraddha (unwavering faith in the Sadguru) and Saburi (patient endurance through life's trials). Embedding this twin formula in the kavach's directional protection ensures that the chanter not only receives external protection but also internalises the inner sadhana of faith and patience. The kavach thus functions simultaneously as protective armour and as a meditation on Sai's core upadesha. (Sources: Shri Sai Satcharitra Chapter 19; Sai Devotional Tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Sai Kavacham be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the day universally dedicated to Shirdi Sai Baba in modern Sai devotional practice — Thursday is sacred to the Guru-tattva, and Sai Baba is revered as the Sadguru-svarupa of the age. The kavach is especially chanted on Guru Purnima (Ashadha Purnima), on Sai Baba's Mahasamadhi anniversary (Vijayadashami, marking 15 October 1918 when Baba left his body), on Ramnavami (since Sai's annual Urus festival at Shirdi is traditionally celebrated on Ramnavami), and during the daily morning Kakad Aarti and evening Shej Aarti at home altars. The phalashruti recommends Yah pathet pratar utthaya — recitation upon waking in the morning — for deliverance from all calamities. (Sources: Shri Sai Satcharitra tradition; Shirdi Sansthan calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of Sai Kavacham promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti (verse 8) declares Yah pathet pratar utthaya Sai Kavacham uttamam, sarva-sankata-mukto bhut Sailokam sa gachchhati — the devotee who recites this excellent Sai Kavacham upon waking in the morning becomes free of all calamities (sankata) and ultimately reaches Sailoka (the abode of Sai). Verse 7 cautions that no Sai stotra recitation succeeds (na sidhyati) without the prior recitation of this kavach — establishing it as the foundational protective recitation that should precede Sai Satcharitra parayana, Sai Ashtottara and Sai Aarti. Devotees report particular benefit in matters of health, family harmony, financial difficulties, court cases and protection from accidents, consistent with Sai Baba's historically recorded miracles. (Sources: Sai Kavacham phalashruti; Shri Sai Satcharitra.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Sai Kavacham relate to Sai Satcharitra and other Sai prayers?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Kavacham is the protective armour-prayer used to open daily Sai sadhana, while the Shri Sai Satcharitra by Hemadpant (Govind Raghunath Dabholkar, 1858-1929) is the principal hagiographical scripture in fifty-three chapters narrating Sai Baba's life and miracles, traditionally read in a saptaha (seven-day) parayana. The Sai Ashtottara Shata Namavali lists 108 names of Sai for daily archana. The Sai Chalisa is a Hindi forty-verse devotional composed in the Tulsidasi Awadhi metre. The Sai Kavacham comes first as protection, the Satcharitra as scripture, the Ashtottara as namavali, and the Chalisa as accessible devotion — together forming the complete daily Sai upasana cycle for devotees worldwide. (Sources: Shirdi Sansthan publications; Sai Devotional Tradition.)
